message:
chore(beads): file frankentorch-ytlg — tensor_inner severed from autograd

Records a finding from a /reality-check pass (continuing from the
sirh fix): `tensor_inner` at `lib.rs:15480` is severed from autograd.

Current behavior (the bug):
  - Forward extracts raw f64 values from both inputs.
  - Computes the last-dim contraction
        c[..a, j_a, ..b, j_b] = sum_l a[..a, l] * b[..b, l]
    in plain f64 outside any autograd primitive.
  - Rebuilds the result as a `requires_grad=false` leaf, which
    silently breaks backward through `torch.inner`.
  - PyTorch's `torch.inner` is differentiable, so this is a
    correctness gap, not a feature gap.

Proposed fix (recorded in the bead body, not implemented yet):
  - 1-D case: delegate to `tensor_dot` (already autograd-aware).
  - Higher-D: reshape both inputs to 2-D — a -> (a_outer, a_last),
    b -> (b_outer, b_last) — compute `matmul(a_flat, b_flat.T)`,
    reshape to the inner output shape. `matmul`, `transpose`, and
    `reshape` are all already autograd-aware, so composition
    through them inherits the existing gradients with no new
    backward function required.

Status: open / priority 2 / type bug. No source change in this
commit; this is a beads-only entry so the issue is queryable via
`br show frankentorch-ytlg` / `br ready` and survives across
sessions.

Untracked `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` is intentionally omitted
(local agent state, ephemeral).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
